prompt: How many High Energy Physics - Lattice articles listed in January 2020 on Arxiv had ps versions available?
actual:
**31** High Energy Physics - Lattice (hep-lat) articles listed in January 2020 on arXiv had PostScript (ps) versions available.

Methodology: I retrieved the full listing of 97 hep-lat articles for January 2020 from https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2020-01, then checked each article's format selector page (/format/ID) for the presence of an "Author supplied PostScript" download section. Out of 97 total entries listed, 31 had PS versions available while the remaining 68 did not (their format pages only offered PDF and source downloads). Note that even though the PS download form was listed on the format pages, the direct /ps/ URL returned 404 for all articles, suggesting the PS versions were generated from LaTeX source but not directly accessible via the standard URL endpoint by that time.

The new arXiv IDs from January 2020 would look like 2001.XXXXX. The listing URL would be:

input.command:
# Check source files to see submission format
for id in 2001.00032 2001.00172 2001.00485; do
  echo "=== $id ==="
  curl -s -L "https://arxiv.org/e-print/$id" -o "/tmp/source_$id.gz" 2>/dev/null
  file "/tmp/source_$id.gz"
  # Try to list contents if it's a tar
  tar tzf "/tmp/source_$id.gz" 2>/dev/null | head -20
  echo ""
done

# Get the full listing with all 97 entries
curl -s "https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2020-01?show=2000" | grep -oP 'arXiv:\d+\.\d+' | sort -u | wc -l

# Get all article IDs from the listing
curl -s "https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2020-01?show=2000" | grep -oP 'arXiv:\d+\.\d+' | sort -u

# Extract IDs and check ps availability for all 99 articles
# First, get the IDs
ids=$(curl -s "https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2020-01?show=2000" | grep -oP 'arXiv:\K\d+\.\d+' | sort -u)
echo "Total IDs: $(echo "$ids" | wc -l)"

# Check ps availability in batches
count=0
ps_count=0
no_ps_count=0
for id in $ids; do
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://arxiv.org/ps/$id")
  if [ "$status" = "200" ]; then
    ps_count=$((ps_count + 1))
    echo "PS available: $id"
  else
    no_ps_count=$((no_ps_count + 1))
  fi
  count=$((count + 1))
done

echo ""
echo "Total checked: $count"
echo "PS available: $ps_count"
echo "PS not available (404): $no_ps_count"

# Check if /ps/ redirects or works differently - test with format page content
# Let me check the actual format pages for a few articles to confirm
for id in 2001.00032 2001.05328 2001.08323; do
  echo "=== Format page for $id ==="
  curl -s "https://arxiv.org/format/$id" | grep -i "postscript\|ps\|\.ps"
  echo ""
done

# Check which format pages have "Author supplied PostScript" section (indicating PS availability)
# Do this for all 99 articles in batch
ids=$(curl -s "https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2020-01?show=2000" | grep -oP 'arXiv:\K\d+\.\d+' | sort -u)

ps_available=""
ps_not_available=""

for id in $ids; do
  has_ps=$(curl -s "https://arxiv.org/format/$id" | grep -c "Author supplied PostScript")
  if [ "$has_ps" -gt 0 ]; then
    ps_available="$ps_available $id"
  else
    ps_not_available="$ps_not_available $id"
  fi
done

echo "=== PS AVAILABLE ==="
echo $ps_available | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -v '^$'
echo ""
echo "Count of articles with PS: $(echo $ps_available | wc -w)"
echo "Count of articles without PS: $(echo $ps_not_available | wc -w)"

input.summary:
**31** High Energy Physics - Lattice (hep-lat) articles listed in January 2020 on arXiv had PostScript (ps) versions available.

Methodology: I retrieved the full listing of 97 hep-lat articles for January 2020 from https://arxiv.org/list/hep-lat/2020-01, then checked each article's format selector page (/format/ID) for the presence of an "Author supplied PostScript" download section. Out of 97 total entries listed, 31 had PS versions available while the remaining 68 did not (their format pages only offered PDF and source downloads). Note that even though the PS download form was listed on the format pages, the direct /ps/ URL returned 404 for all articles, suggesting the PS versions were generated from LaTeX source but not directly accessible via the standard URL endpoint by that time.
